svg.aria-label: Two bruising modes matter to a harvester: quasi-static grip damage, where sustained contact pressure exceeds the tissue failure pressure and the controlling variable is peak contact pressure, not total force; and dynamic impact damage, where a drop delivers kinetic energy and the controlling variable is impact energy, which sets a maximum safe placement height and is why the mission lowers fruit rather than dropping it.
svg.text: Two damage modes: quasi-static vs impact
lever: contact area, compliance -> how the
force is applied matters as much as how much.
a drop or hard placement delivers kinetic
energy to a small contact region.
sets a maximum safe placement height ->
the mission LOWERS fruit, never drops it.
Bruising is mechanical damage: cells rupture when local stress exceeds their failure limit, causing internal softening that may appear only days
after harvest. Both modes become design bounds, the grip-force window (Section 7.4) and the placement-height limit (Section 7.4). Section 6.2.
